ListItem.Para: Associated low limit valve is deactivated open.
Opposite condenser inlet temperature sensor system operates normally.
Opposite air conditioning pack operates normally.
Opposite cabin air compressors operate normally.
Opposite trim air system operates normally.

Note.Para: For an air conditioning pack to be operating normally, certain items associated with that system must operate normally. See MEL item 21-51-01 for a list of those items.
For a cabin air compressor to be operating normally, certain items must operate normally. See MEL item 21-52-01 for a list of those items.
For a trim air system to operate normally, certain items must operate normally. See MEL item 21-51-01 for a list of those items.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the associated low limit valve open (AMM DDG 21-52-05).

ListItem.Para: For left low limit valve:
Set left air conditioning pack OFF.
Set the GND TEST selector to ENABLE no less than 30-seconds prior to opening the Pack Control Unit circuit breakers.
Open the following circuit breakers and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
PACK CTRL UNIT- CHAN L1
PACK CTRL UNIT- CHAN L2
Gain access to left low limit valve.
Disconnect, cap and stow low limit valve electrical connector.
Use the manual open/close knob to set valve in open position.

Note.Para: Locking the low limit valve open may result in display of the associated LOW LIMIT VALVE L status message.

ListItem.Para: Gain access to left condenser inlet temperature sensor.
Disconnect, cap and stow temperature sensor electrical connector.

Note.Para: The CONDENSER INLET SENSOR L status messages will be displayed.

ListItem.Para: Close the following circuit breakers:
PACK CTRL UNIT- CHAN L1
PACK CTRL UNIT- CHAN L2
Return the GND TEST selector to NORM.
Set pack as desired.

Note.Para: Associated PACK or PACK MODE advisory message may be displayed

ListItem.Para: For right low limit valve:
Select right air conditioning pack OFF.
Set the GND TEST selector to ENABLE no less than 30-seconds prior to opening the Pack Control Unit circuit breakers.
Open the following circuit breakers and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
PACK CTRL UNIT- CHAN R1
PACK CTRL UNIT- CHAN R2
Gain access to right low limit valve.
Disconnect, cap and stow low limit valve electrical connector.
Use the manual open/close knob to set valve in open position.

Note.Para: Locking the low limit valve open may result in display of the associated LOW LIMIT VALVE R status message.

ListItem.Para: Gain access to right condenser inlet temperature sensor.
Disconnect, cap and stow temperature sensor electrical connector.

Note.Para: The CONDENSER INLET SENSOR R status messages will be displayed.

ListItem.Para: Close the following circuit breakers:
PACK CTRL UNIT- CHAN R1
PACK CTRL UNIT- CHAN R2
Return the GND TEST selector to NORM.
Set pack as desired.

Note.Para: Associated PACK or PACK MODE advisory message may be displayed

Associated PACK MODE or PACK advisory message may be displayed before engine start. After engine start, the associated PACK MODE advisory message will be displayed. The pack with the inoperative low limit valve will remain OFF on the ground and at low altitudes with the PACK MODE advisory message displayed and PACK OFF light illuminated. The pack will operate in standby cooling mode when appropriate altitude and OAT conditions exist with the PACK MODE advisory message displayed and PACK OFF light extinguished.

If the opposite pack fails inflight, the pack operating in standby cooling mode will continue to operate regardless of altitude or OAT to maintain cabin pressurization. A pack operating in standby cooling mode is capable of maintaining cabin pressurization at cruise altitude.

On takeoff, the PACK ALTITUDE LIMIT caution message may appear anytime after liftoff until 1,000 feet above airport elevation. The message may be displayed for up to 2 minutes. On landing, this message may appear at altitudes less than 1000 feet above airport elevation until touchdown.

ListItem.Para: If cabin temperatures become warmer than desired, consider one or more of the following temporary actions:
Select IFE system modes designed to reduce cabin heat loads.

Note.Para: Availability of modes dependent on IFE system installed and airline modifiable options.

ListItem.Para: Set IFE off using the IFE Power switch on the Video Control Station.
Select HEAT REDUCTION mode using the Cabin Attendant Panel TEMPERATURE menu (adjusts cabin lighting and window shading).
If on ground, set FCAC OFF.
Associated low limit valve is deactivated open.
Opposite low limit valve operates normally.
Opposite air conditioning pack operates normally.
Opposite cabin air compressors operate normally.
Opposite trim air system operates normally.

Note.Para: Associated PACK MODE or PACK advisory message may be displayed before engine start. After engine start, the associated PACK MODE advisory message will be displayed. The pack with the inoperative condenser inlet temperature sensor system will remain OFF on the ground and at low altitudes with the PACK MODE advisory message displayed and PACK OFF light illuminated. The pack will operate in standby cooling mode when appropriate altitude and OAT conditions exist with the PACK MODE advisory message displayed and PACK OFF light extinguished.

If the opposite pack fails inflight, the pack operating in standby cooling mode will continue to operate regardless of altitude or OAT to maintain cabin pressurization. A pack operating in standby cooling mode is capable of maintaining cabin pressurization at cruise altitude.

On takeoff, the PACK ALTITUDE LIMIT caution message may appear anytime after liftoff until 1,000 feet above airport elevation. The message may be displayed for up to 2 minutes. On landing, this message may appear at altitudes less than 1000 feet above airport elevation until touchdown.

ListItem.Para: If cabin temperatures become warmer than desired, consider one or more of the following temporary actions:
Select IFE system modes designed to reduce cabin heat loads.
Set IFE off using the IFE Power switch on the Video Control Station.
Select HEAT REDUCTION mode using the Cabin Attendant Panel TEMPERATURE menu (adjusts cabin lighting and window shading).
If on ground, set FCAC OFF.
